name: The col de Latrape by the two pans
name_original: Le col de Latrape par les deux pans
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aulus-Les-Bains are situated in the heart of the Ariège Pyrenees, an area characterized by challenging mountain passes and diverse landscapes. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes traversing valleys, dense forests, and open mountain terrain. Cyclists encounter iconic Pyrenean cols, offering varied gradients and panoramic views of the surrounding peaks and natural features. Best road cycling routes around Aulus-Les-Bains The most popular road cycling route is The col de Latrape by the two pans, a 17.6 miles (28.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route features the ascent of Col de Latrape, a well-known Pyrenean pass. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Col de Port – Lers Pass loop from L'Artigue, a difficult 59.2 miles (95.3 km) path. This route combines the Col de Port and Port de Lers, offering extensive mountain views and varied terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Agnès Pass – Lers Pass loop from Aulus-les-Bains, a 42.3 miles (68.1 km) trail leading through challenging mountain passes, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Aulus-Les-Bains is defined by challenging mountain passes, scenic valleys, and diverse Pyrenean landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate climbs to difficult, long-distance ascents. The routes in Aulus-Les-Bains are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 2,300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aulus-Les-Bains's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: The Aulus-Les-Bains region offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 25 tours available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate climbs to challenging, long-distance ascents through the Pyrenees.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced road cyclists expect in the Aulus-Les-Bains region?
A: Advanced road cyclists will find numerous challenging mountain passes, many of which are iconic Tour de France climbs. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20533050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Port – Lers Pass loop from L'Artigue&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39356925" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Agnès Pass – Lers Pass loop from Aulus-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and extensive mountain views, providing a demanding experience.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes starting from Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: Yes, Aulus-Les-Bains is an excellent base for circular road cycling routes. You can find structured loops that combine several famous cols, such as the "Col d'Agnès and Col de Port Loop" or the "Col de la Core – Col de Latrape loop." An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41542827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Core Pass – Seix loop from Aulus-les-Bains&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse landscapes.

Q: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see while road cycling near Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You'll encounter breathtaking landscapes, including the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Agnès&lt;/a&gt; with its superb panoramas, the tranquil Garbet Valley, and open views at the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211843" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port de Lers&lt;/a&gt;. The magnificent Cascade d'Ars, one of the most beautiful waterfalls in the Pyrenees, is also nearby.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in the Aulus-Les-Bains area?
A: The best time for road cycling in Aulus-Les-Bains is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, mountain passes are typically clear of snow, and the weather is more favorable for tackling the challenging ascents and enjoying the scenic routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options around Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: While many routes feature challenging mountain passes, there are options for families. Look for routes with lower average gradients or those that stick to valley roads. The region offers a few easier routes, and you can find sections that are less demanding, allowing for a more relaxed family cycling experience.

Q: Is road cycling around Aulus-Les-Bains suitable for cyclists with dogs?
A: Bringing dogs on road cycling routes requires careful consideration, especially in mountainous terrain and protected areas like the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Nature Park. While specific regulations vary, it's generally advisable to ensure your dog is fit for the activity and to keep them on a leash where required. Always carry enough water and be mindful of traffic on roads.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities for road cycling in Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: Aulus-Les-Bains, being a hub for outdoor activities, typically offers parking options within the village. You can usually find public parking areas suitable for starting your cycling tours. It's advisable to check local signage for any specific parking regulations or designated spots for cyclists.

Q: Can I reach Aulus-Les-Bains with public transport if I'm bringing my bike?
A: Public transport options in mountainous regions like the Ariège Pyrenees can be limited, especially for carrying bikes. While some regional bus services might allow bikes, it's best to verify with the specific transport provider beforehand. Many cyclists choose to travel by car to Aulus-Les-Bains to ensure easy transport of their equipment.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the challenging mountain passes, the stunning Pyrenean scenery, and the diverse terrain that makes every ride unique. The sense of accomplishment after conquering iconic cols is a common highlight.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes that include a famous pass?
A: Yes, for those looking for a moderate challenge that still includes a well-known Pyrenean pass, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945185790/the-col-de-latrape-by-the-two-pans" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The col de Latrape by the two pans&lt;/a&gt; is a great option. This route features the ascent of Col de Latrape and is considered moderate, making it accessible for a wider range of cyclists.

Q: What is the average difficulty of road cycling routes in Aulus-Les-Bains?
A: The road cycling routes around Aulus-Les-Bains are predominantly challenging, with 15 out of 28 routes rated as 'difficult' and 12 as 'moderate'. Only a small number of routes are considered 'easy', reflecting the mountainous nature of the Ariège Pyrenees and its iconic cols.
tips: Apart from its interest for cyclists, this pass is in winter the departure of a ski lift to the station of Guzet-Neige.
